political.s: FEC: $109K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 9 executive donors.

charity.s: $7M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.03% of revenue).

dei.s: HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 351:1 (median employee pay $101,004) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.extract: Booking Holdings Inc. is an American travel technology company based in Norwalk, Connecticut, that owns and operates several travel fare aggregators and travel fare metasearch engines including namesake and flagship Booking.com, Priceline.com, Agoda, Kayak, Cheapflights, Rentalcars.com, Momondo, and OpenTable. It operates websites in about 40 languages and 200 countries.

Epi's Canoe & Kayak Team, LLC v. State of Texas

health.s: 5 CPSC recalls (1 in past 24 months); latest hazard: the recalled kayak paddles can break during use.

governance.s: Wikidata: owned by BlackRock; HQ Norwalk. (Wikidata Q18674747)

topHazards.label: The recalled kayak paddles can break during use, posing a drowning hazard.
The length adjustment buckles release unexpectedly, causing the item being stored to fall and injure people nearby
The cart's plastic wheel rims can burst when the rubber tires are over-inflated, posing an injury hazard to consumers.
The chinstrap buckle can fail, posing a head injury hazard to users.
The plastic tubes used to inflate the paddle float could break and deflate, posing a drowning hazard to consumers.

sampleRecalls.title: Werner Paddles Recalls Kayak Paddles Bracelets Due to Drowning Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves models Stealth and Covert kayak paddles. The brand name Werner appears on decals on both blades and the model name is printed on the right paddle blade. The paddles are made of black carbon fiber.
hazards: The recalled kayak paddles can break during use, posing a drowning hazard.
injuries: The firm has received 80 reports of the paddles breaking during use. No injuries have been reported.
units: About 700 (In addition, about 25 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: Attwood Recalls Kayak and Watersports Storage Hanger Due to Injury Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves kayak and watersports storage hangers sold as a pair of blue and black fabric straps that measure approximately 1 inch wide and 84 inches long when unbuckled. The straps include a plastic snap buckle to adjust the length and a plastic-coated steel S-hook attachment for hanging. Model number 11917-…
hazards: The length adjustment buckles release unexpectedly, causing the item being stored to fall and injure people nearby

sampleRecalls.title: L.L.Bean Recalls Boat Carts for Canoes and Kayaks; Plastic Wheel Rims Can Burst and Injure Users
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves L.L.Bean's Deluxe Packaway Boat Carts used to haul canoes and kayaks into or out of the water by hand. The carts have a white and blue aluminum frame with rubber tires and have two black nylon straps marked L.L.Bean. The carts weigh about seven pounds.
hazards: The cart's plastic wheel rims can burst when the rubber tires are over-inflated, posing an injury hazard to consumers.
injuries: L.L.Bean has received two reports of the plastic wheel rims on the cart bursting, resulting in bruises to a consumer who was struck by broken, flying pieces. No injuries were reported in the second i…

sampleRecalls.title: WRSI Recalls Kayaking and Rafting Helmets Due to Head Injury Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves WRSI Moment and Trident models whitewater kayaking and rafting helmets. They have a black chinstrap with a black plastic buckle and were sold in sizes S/M and M/L. "WRSI" is printed under or on top of the helmet's visor. WRSI's "Wave Logo" is visible on the back of the Moment helmet. "WRSI" is pri…
hazards: The chinstrap buckle can fail, posing a head injury hazard to users.
injuries: WRSI is aware of 10 incidents of chinstrap failures. No injuries have been reported.
units: About 2,000 in the United States and 200 in Canada

sampleRecalls.title: Kayak Paddle Floats Recalled by NRS Due to Drowning Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: The NRS Sea Kayak Paddle Float is an inflatable device that is attached to one side of the paddle to help the kayaker re-enter the kayak in open water. The paddle float is yellow and black and measures 15" wide by 28" long, before inflation. The NRS logo is on one side. This recall involves those paddle floats with in…
hazards: The plastic tubes used to inflate the paddle float could break and deflate, posing a drowning hazard to consumers.
injuries: NRS has received five reports of paddle floats breaking and deflating. No injuries have been reported.
